title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market insights as of Friday, July 10, 2026 close. Not financial advice.

items.headline: AI Enthusiasm Fuels Market Gains Ahead of Earnings Season
items.summary: U.S. stocks edged higher on Friday, concluding a week where investor sentiment was largely driven by continued enthusiasm for artificial intelligence innovations and anticipation of the upcoming earnings season. Major indices posted modest gains, with the S&P 500 marking its fourth winning week in the last five. Despite mixed economic data, growth-oriented sectors, particularly technology, continued to demonstrate leadership.

items.statContext: +0.42% (+31.75 points) on Friday. Up 1.2% for the week.
+0.29% (+74.72 points) on Friday. Up 1.7% for the week.
+0.29% (+149.60 points) on Friday. Down 0.5% for the week.

items.headline: SK Hynix Soars on Nasdaq Debut, Boosts AI Trade
items.summary: South Korean semiconductor giant SK Hynix saw its stock surge by 12.76% to $168.01 on its Nasdaq debut, re-energizing the broader AI trade. The company raised approximately $26.5 billion in its share sale.

items.headline: WD-40 Climbs on Strong Q3 Earnings, Raised Guidance
items.summary: WD-40 Co. ($WDFC) jumped 10.7% after reporting stronger-than-expected third-quarter profits and raising its full-year fiscal guidance, signaling robust operational performance.

items.headline: Nvidia Extends Gains as AI Momentum Continues
items.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) continued its strong performance, rising 4.0% on Friday. The company remains a key driver of the AI boom, significantly influencing the S&P 500's upward trend.

items.headline: Moderna Dips Amid Broader Health Sector Weakness
items.summary: Moderna Inc. ($MRNA) saw a significant decline of 11.30% on Friday, as the health sector generally bucked the market's positive trend and ended in the red.

items.headline: Delta Air Lines Falls Despite Beating Q2 Expectations
items.summary: Delta Air Lines ($DAL) shares fell 1.81% on Friday, despite the company reporting second-quarter financial results that exceeded Wall Street expectations and reinstating its full-year fiscal guidance.

items.headline: Capital Rotation Seen in Q2 as Industrials, Healthcare, and Financials Outperform Tech
items.summary: While AI and technology led much of the first half of 2026, the latter half of Q2 saw a notable rotation of capital. The Technology sector experienced a decline of -3.3% in June, while Industrials, Healthcare, and Financials outperformed, suggesting a broadening of market leadership.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Reaffirms Commitment to Price Stability
items.summary: The Federal Reserve delivered its semi-annual Monetary Policy Report to Congress, reiterating its commitment to achieving price stability. While current inflation metrics remain elevated, policymakers anticipate service-sector price pressures to be transitory.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data Suggests Healthy Economic Slowdown
items.summary: Recent economic data paints a picture of an economy that continues to slow at a healthy pace without falling into recession. Services activity remained in expansion territory, weekly jobless claims came in better than expected, and job openings continue to demonstrate a resilient labor market, though manufacturing data was softer.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Begins Next Week with Major Bank Reports
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season is set to kick off next week, with several major financial institutions, including Bank of America ($BAC), Citigroup ($C), Goldman Sachs ($GS), JPMorgan Chase ($JPM), and Wells Fargo ($WFC), slated to report results before the market open on Tuesday. These reports are expected to provide critical insights into net interest margins, loan growth, and credit quality.
